events.title: Brian May turns 79
events.blurb: Queen's legendary guitarist, Brian May, celebrates his 79th birthday today.

angles.name: Guitar God
angles.copy: Happy 79th birthday to the one and only Brian May! From stadium anthems to astrophysics, what's your favourite Queen track featuring his iconic guitar work?

events.title: Mick Jagger turns 83
events.blurb: The iconic frontman of The Rolling Stones, Mick Jagger, celebrates his 83rd birthday.

angles.name: Jagger's Enduring Energy
angles.copy: Eighty-three years young and still moving like Jagger! Wishing a very happy birthday to the legendary Mick Jagger. What's your go-to Stones track to get you dancing?

events.title: MTV launched (1981)
events.blurb: On this day in 1981, MTV launched, forever changing the music landscape with its first video: The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star'.

angles.name: Video Killed The Radio Star
angles.copy: Forty-five years ago today, MTV burst onto our screens with that famously ironic first video. Did video really kill the radio star, or just give us more ways to love music?

events.title: Oasis released Definitely Maybe (1994)
events.blurb: Oasis's debut album, 'Definitely Maybe', was released on this day in 1994, becoming a cornerstone of Britpop.

angles.name: Britpop Classic
angles.copy: Cast your mind back to '94! Oasis dropped 'Definitely Maybe' on this day, kicking off a Britpop revolution. What's your favourite track from that iconic debut?

events.title: Woodstock Festival took place (1969)
events.blurb: The legendary Woodstock Music & Art Fair, a pivotal moment in music history, concluded on this day in 1969.

angles.name: Peace, Love & Music
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years ago, half a million people gathered for three days of peace, love, and music at Woodstock. Which iconic performance from the festival still resonates with you?

events.title: Anniversary of Elvis Presley's Death
events.blurb: Today marks the anniversary of the passing of Elvis Presley, who would have been 91 this year.

angles.name: Remembering The King
angles.copy: Remembering The King, Elvis Presley, whose music continues to captivate generations. His legacy would have been 91 this year. What's your favourite Elvis track?

events.title: Anniversary of Tupac Shakur's Death
events.blurb: On this day, we remember the tragic passing of Tupac Shakur in Las Vegas, who would have been 55 this year.

angles.name: Tupac's Legacy
angles.copy: Remembering the profound impact of Tupac Shakur, a voice that shaped a generation, who would have been 55 today. His music and message live on.

events.title: Anniversary of Jimi Hendrix's Death
events.blurb: Today marks the anniversary of the death of guitar legend Jimi Hendrix, who would have been 84 this year.

angles.name: Guitar Legend Remembered
angles.copy: Remembering the unparalleled genius of Jimi Hendrix, who left us too soon but whose music lives forever. He would have been 84 this year. What's your favourite Hendrix riff?

events.title: The Beatles released Abbey Road (1969)
events.blurb: The Beatles' iconic album 'Abbey Road' was released on this day in 1969, featuring that legendary zebra crossing cover.

angles.name: Iconic Album Cover
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years ago, The Beatles dropped 'Abbey Road', an album as iconic as its cover. What's your favourite track from their final recorded masterpiece?

events.title: Chrissie Hynde turns 75
events.blurb: The formidable lead singer of The Pretenders, Chrissie Hynde, celebrates her 75th birthday today.

angles.name: Pretenders Icon
angles.copy: Happy 75th birthday to the incredible Chrissie Hynde! A true rock icon with a voice that commands attention. What's your favourite Pretenders hit?

events.title: Liam Gallagher turns 54
events.blurb: The distinctive voice of Oasis and a Britpop icon, Liam Gallagher, celebrates his 54th birthday today.

angles.name: Mad For It
angles.copy: You know the voice, you know the attitude! Happy 54th birthday to the one and only Liam Gallagher. What's your favourite moment from his career?

events.title: John Lennon released Imagine (1971)
events.blurb: John Lennon's timeless anthem for peace, 'Imagine', was released on this day in 1971.

angles.name: Imagine Peace
angles.copy: Fifty-five years since John Lennon asked us to 'Imagine'. A song that still resonates globally. What does this iconic track mean to you?

events.title: Apple released the first iPod (2001)
events.blurb: On this day in 2001, Apple introduced the first iPod, revolutionising how we carry and listen to music.

angles.name: The Original iPod
angles.copy: Twenty-five years ago, the original iPod dropped and changed everything. Remember stuffing all your CDs onto one device? What was the first album you loaded onto yours?

events.title: James Bond Day
events.blurb: October 5th is celebrated annually as James Bond Day, marking the release date of the first Bond film, 'Dr. No', in 1962.

angles.name: Shaken, Not Stirred
angles.copy: Happy James Bond Day! From iconic gadgets to unforgettable theme tunes, what's your favourite 007 moment or Bond actor?

events.title: John Lennon would have been 86
events.blurb: Remembering the legendary John Lennon, who would have celebrated his 86th birthday today.

angles.name: Lennon's Legacy
angles.copy: On what would have been his 86th birthday, we celebrate the enduring legacy of John Lennon. His music and message continue to inspire. What's your favourite Lennon song?

events.title: John Cleese turns 87
events.blurb: The iconic British comedian and actor, John Cleese, celebrates his 87th birthday today.

angles.name: Comedy Genius
angles.copy: Wishing a very happy 87th birthday to the incomparable John Cleese! From Monty Python to Fawlty Towers, what's your favourite moment from this British comedy legend?

events.title: The Beatles released 'White Album' (1968)
events.blurb: The Beatles' experimental double album, commonly known as 'The White Album', was released on this day in 1968.

angles.name: The White Album
angles.copy: Fifty-eight years ago today, The Beatles unleashed their ambitious 'White Album'. A sprawling masterpiece of diverse tracks. What's your hidden gem from this double LP?
